mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 200k signals robust labor demand and future inflation pressure, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to rise as markets price in higher long-term inflation expectations and reduced Fed accommodation.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 136167k as of May 2014 indicates strong labor market momentum
regime_hint: post-crisis recovery with tightening labor slack
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of instances where PAYEMS growth exceeds 200k month-over-month, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 0.39% represents the tail end of zero-lower-bound policy. When FEDFUNDS rises above 0.5% in a single month, it signals the Fed has begun normalization, causing 10-year Treasury yields to rise as markets reprice the duration risk and expected path of future short rates.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises above 0.5% in a single month
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.39% as of May 2014, near historical lows
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls in > 60% of instances following FEDFUNDS crossing above 0.5%, or the yield change is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) over a 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) at 791k indicate strong residential demand. When HOUST rises above 900k, it signals accelerating construction activity and future demand-driven inflation, causing 10-year Treasury yields to rise as inflation expectations increase and the Fed is perceived as needing to tighten policy.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises above 900k in a single month
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with accelerating housing demand
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls in > 55% of instances where HOUST exceeds 900k, or the predictive power decays below Rank IC 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and core inflation (CPIAUCSL) jointly signal supply-side tightness. When INDPRO growth accelerates while CPIAUCSL remains elevated, it indicates the economy is operating near capacity with limited slack, forcing the Fed to maintain higher real rates and causing 10-year Treasury yields to rise.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth exceeds 0.5% AND CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 2.0%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 106.12 and CPIAUCSL at 216.71 as of May 2014
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the joint signal, or the interaction term's coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in a logistic regression over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) at 6.7% is elevated relative to pre-crisis levels. When UNRATE falls below 6.0%, it signals labor market normalization and reduced economic slack, causing 10-year Treasury yields to rise as the Fed is perceived as needing to tighten to prevent overheating.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 6.0% in a single month
regime_hint: labor market normalization and slack reduction
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls in > 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 6.0%, or the relationship reverses (negative correlation) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly measure consumer and residential demand. When both accelerate simultaneously (RSAFS growth > 0.4% month-over-month AND HOUST growth > 0.3% month-over-month), it signals broad-based demand strength that pressures inflation and causes 10-year Treasury yields to rise.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth exceeds 0.4% AND HOUST month-over-month growth exceeds 0.3%
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 355655M and HOUST at 791k as of April-May 2014
regime_hint: synchronized demand acceleration in consumer and housing sectors
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the joint signal's predictive power (Rank IC) falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-crisis recovery regime, the spread between inflation (CPIAUCSL year-over-year) and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) determines real monetary policy stance. When this spread widens (inflation rising faster than FEDFUNDS), it signals monetary accommodation is ending, causing 10-year Treasury yields to rise as the market reprices the duration of low-rate policy.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds FEDFUNDS by more than 1.5 percentage points
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.39% and CPIAUCSL at 216.71 as of May 2014
regime_hint: post-crisis recovery with real rates deeply negative
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls in > 55% of instances where the spread widens, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in a 2-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) is a leading indicator of supply-side capacity utilization. When INDPRO decelerates (month-over-month growth falls below 0.2%), it signals slowing economic momentum and reduced inflation pressure, causing 10-year Treasury yields to fall as the Fed is perceived as maintaining accommodative policy longer.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in > 60% of instances where INDPRO growth falls below 0.2%, or the predictive power (Rank IC) is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
